11	var GatedPodsGauge prometheus.Gauge
12	var onceCommon sync.Once
13	
14	func initCommonMetrics() {
15		onceCommon.Do(func() {
16			GatedPodsGauge = prometheus.NewGauge(
17				prometheus.GaugeOpts{
18					Name: "mto_ppo_pods_gated",
19					Help: "The current number of gated pods (this metric is not considered reliable yet)",
20				},
21			)
22			metrics2.Registry.MustRegister(GatedPodsGauge)
23		})

1	package metrics
2	
3	import (
4		"sync"
5	
6		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils"
7	
8		metrics2 "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics"
9	
10		"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
11	)
12	
13	var (
14		TimeToProcessPod        prometheus.Histogram
15		TimeToProcessGatedPod   prometheus.Histogram
16		TimeToInspectImage      prometheus.Histogram
17		TimeToInspectPodImages  prometheus.Histogram
18		ProcessedPodsCtrl       prometheus.Counter
19		FailedInspectionCounter prometheus.Counter
20	)
21	
22	var onceController sync.Once
23	
24	func InitPodPlacementControllerMetrics() {
25		onceController.Do(initPodPlacementControllerMetrics)
26	}
27	
28	func initPodPlacementControllerMetrics() {
29		initCommonMetrics()
30		TimeToProcessPod = prometheus.NewHistogram(
31			prometheus.HistogramOpts{
32				Name:    "mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_pod_seconds",
33				Help:    "Time taken to process any pod",
34				Buckets: utils.Buckets(),
35			},
36		)
37		TimeToProcessGatedPod = prometheus.NewHistogram(
38			prometheus.HistogramOpts{
39				Name:    "mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_gated_pod_seconds",
40				Help:    "Time taken to process a pod that is gated (includes inspection)",
41				Buckets: utils.Buckets(),
42			},
43		)
44		TimeToInspectImage = prometheus.NewHistogram(
45			prometheus.HistogramOpts{
46				Name:    "mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_image_seconds",
47				Help:    "Time taken to inspect an image",
48				Buckets: utils.Buckets(),
49			},
50		)
51		TimeToInspectPodImages = prometheus.NewHistogram(
52			prometheus.HistogramOpts{
53				Name:    "mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_pod_images_seconds",
54				Help:    "The time taken to inspect all the images in a pod (all containers)",
55				Buckets: utils.Buckets(),
56			},
57		)
58		ProcessedPodsCtrl = prometheus.NewCounter(
59			prometheus.CounterOpts{
60				Name: "mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total",
61				Help: "The total number of pods processed by the pod placement controller that had a scheduling gate",
62			},
63		)
64		FailedInspectionCounter = prometheus.NewCounter(
65			prometheus.CounterOpts{
66				Name: "mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total",
67				Help: "The total number of image inspections that failed",
68			},
69		)
70		metrics2.Registry.MustRegister(TimeToProcessPod, TimeToProcessGatedPod, TimeToInspectImage,
71			TimeToInspectPodImages, ProcessedPodsCtrl, FailedInspectionCounter)
72	}
73

1	package metrics

3	import (
4		"sync"
5	
6		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils"

8		metrics2 "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics"

12	
13	var (
14		ProcessedPodsWH prometheus.Counter
15		GatedPods       prometheus.Counter
16		ResponseTime    prometheus.Histogram
17	)
18	
19	var onceWebhook sync.Once
20	
21	func InitWebhookMetrics() {
22		onceWebhook.Do(initWebhookMetrics)
23	}
24	
25	func initWebhookMetrics() {
26		initCommonMetrics()
27		ProcessedPodsWH = prometheus.NewCounter(
28			prometheus.CounterOpts{
29				Name: "mto_ppo_wh_pods_processed_total",
30				Help: "The total number of pods processed by the webhook",
31			},
32		)
33		GatedPods = prometheus.NewCounter(
34			prometheus.CounterOpts{
35				Name: "mto_ppo_wh_pods_gated_total",
36				Help: "The total number of pods gated by the webhook",
37			},
38		)
39	
40		ResponseTime = prometheus.NewHistogram(
41			prometheus.HistogramOpts{
42				Name:    "mto_ppo_wh_response_time_seconds",
43				Help:    "The response time of the webhook",
44				Buckets: utils.Buckets(),
45			},
46		)
47		metrics2.Registry.MustRegister(ProcessedPodsWH, GatedPods, ResponseTime)
48	}

17	package image
18	
19	import (
20		"context"
21		"crypto/sha256"
22		"encoding/hex"
23		"time"
24	
25		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/image/metrics"
26		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils"
27	
28		"github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru/v2/expirable"
29		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets"
30	
31		ctrllog "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log"
32	)
33	
34	type cacheProxy struct {
35		registryInspector IRegistryInspector
36		imageRefsCache    *expirable.LRU[string, sets.Set[string]] // LRU cache with expirable keys
37	}
38	
39	func (c *cacheProxy) GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx context.Context, imageReference string,
40		skipCache bool, secrets [][]byte) (sets.Set[string], error) {
41		metrics.InitCommonMetrics()
42		metrics.InspectionGauge.Set(float64(c.imageRefsCache.Len()))
43		now := time.Now()
44		authJSON, err := marshaledImagePullSecrets(imageReference, secrets)
45		if err != nil {
46			return nil, err
47		}
48	
49		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithValues("imageReference", imageReference)
50		hash := computeHash(imageReference, authJSON)
51		if architectures, ok := c.imageRefsCache.Get(hash); ok && !skipCache {
52			log.V(3).Info("Cache hit", "architectures", architectures, "hash", hash)
53			defer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenHit)
54			return architectures, nil
55		}
56		architectures, err := c.registryInspector.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx, imageReference, true, secrets)
57		if err != nil {
58			return nil, err
59		}
60	
61		log.V(3).Info("Cache miss...adding to cache", "architectures", architectures, "hash", hash)
62		if !skipCache {
63			c.imageRefsCache.Add(hash, architectures)
64		}
65		defer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenMiss)
66		return architectures, nil
67	}
68	
69	func (c *cacheProxy) GetRegistryInspector() IRegistryInspector {
70		return c.registryInspector
71	}
72	
73	// clearCache purges the image metadata cache
74	func (c *cacheProxy) clearCache() {
75		c.imageRefsCache.Purge()
76	}
77	
78	func newCacheProxy() *cacheProxy {
79		return &cacheProxy{
80			registryInspector: newRegistryInspector(),
81			imageRefsCache:    expirable.NewLRU[string, sets.Set[string]](256, nil, time.Hour*6),
82		}
83	}
84	
85	func computeHash(imageReference string, secrets []byte) string {
86		h := sha256.New()
87		h.Write([]byte(imageReference))
88		h.Write([]byte{0})
89		h.Write(secrets)
90		return hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))
91	}
92

17	package image
18	
19	import (
20		"context"
21		"sync"
22	
23		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets"
24	)
25	
26	var (
27		singletonImageFacade *Facade
28		// once is used for lazy initialization of the singletonImageFacade
29		once sync.Once
30	)
31	
32	type Facade struct {
33		inspectionCache       ICache
34		storeGlobalPullSecret func(pullSecret []byte)
35		clearCache            func()
36	}
37	
38	func (i *Facade) GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx context.Context, imageReference string, skipCache bool, secrets [][]byte) (architectures sets.Set[string], err error) {
39		return i.inspectionCache.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx, imageReference, skipCache, secrets)
40	}
41	
42	func (i *Facade) StoreGlobalPullSecret(pullSecret []byte) {
43		i.storeGlobalPullSecret(pullSecret)
44		i.clearCache()
45	}
46	
47	func newImageFacade() *Facade {
48		inspectionCache := newCacheProxy()
49		return &Facade{
50			inspectionCache:       inspectionCache,
51			storeGlobalPullSecret: inspectionCache.registryInspector.storeGlobalPullSecret,
52			clearCache:            inspectionCache.clearCache,
53		}
54	}
55	
56	func FacadeSingleton() *Facade {
57		once.Do(func() {
58			singletonImageFacade = newImageFacade()
59		})
60		return singletonImageFacade
61	}

text:
Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Here's the implementation:

---

## Implementation

### File 1: `pkg/image/metrics/common_metrics.go`

Add the new `*prometheus.CounterVec` variable and register it in `InitCommonMetrics()`.

```go
package metrics

import (
	"sync"

	"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils"

	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
	metrics2 "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics"
)

var onceCommon sync.Once

var (
	InspectionGauge             prometheus.Gauge
	TimeToInspectImageGivenHit  prometheus.Histogram
	TimeToInspectImageGivenMiss prometheus.Histogram
	ImageCacheHitsTotal         *prometheus.CounterVec
)

func InitCommonMetrics() {
	onceCommon.Do(func() {
		InspectionGauge = prometheus.NewGauge(
			prometheus.GaugeOpts{
				Name: "mto_inspection_cache_size",
				Help: "Current size of the MTO inspection cache",
			},
		)
		TimeToInspectImageGivenHit = prometheus.NewHistogram(
			prometheus.HistogramOpts{
				Name:    "mto_cache_hit_processing_duration_seconds",
				Help:    "Duration to process a cache hit for MTO inspection",
				Buckets: utils.Buckets(),
			})
		TimeToInspectImageGivenMiss = prometheus.NewHistogram(
			prometheus.HistogramOpts{
				Name:    "mto_cache_miss_processing_duration_seconds",
				Help:    "Duration to process a cache miss for MTO inspection",
				Buckets: utils.Buckets(),
			})
		ImageCacheHitsTotal = prometheus.NewCounterVec(
			prometheus.CounterOpts{
				Name: "mto_ppo_ctrl_image_cache_hits_total",
				Help: "The total number of image architecture results returned from cache",
			},
			[]string{"image_ref"},
		)

		metrics2.Registry.MustRegister(InspectionGauge, ImageCacheHitsTotal)
	})
}
```

**Changes from original:**
- Added `ImageCacheHitsTotal *prometheus.CounterVec` to the var block (line 18, new)
- Added the `prometheus.NewCounterVec(...)` initialization inside `InitCommonMetrics()` (after `TimeToInspectImageGivenMiss`, before the `MustRegister` call)
- Added `ImageCacheHitsTotal` to the `metrics2.Registry.MustRegister(...)` call

### File 2: `pkg/image/cache.go`

Increment the counter on cache hit, using `imageReference` as the `image_ref` label value.

```go
	if architectures, ok := c.imageRefsCache.Get(hash); ok && !skipCache {
		log.V(3).Info("Cache hit", "architectures", architectures, "hash", hash)
		metrics.ImageCacheHitsTotal.With(prometheus.Labels{"image_ref": imageReference}).Inc()
		defer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenHit)
		return architectures, nil
	}
```

**Change from original:** Added one line after the `log.V(3).Info(...)` call on line 52:
```go
metrics.ImageCacheHitsTotal.With(prometheus.Labels{"image_ref": imageReference}).Inc()
```

This also requires adding `"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"` to the imports in `cache.go`. The full import block becomes:

```go
import (
	"context"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"encoding/hex"
	"time"

	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"

	"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/image/metrics"
	"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils"

	"github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru/v2/expirable"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets"

	ctrllog "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log"
)
```

---

## Summary of all changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `pkg/image/metrics/common_metrics.go` | Declare `ImageCacheHitsTotal *prometheus.CounterVec`, initialize it as `prometheus.NewCounterVec` with label `image_ref`, register it via `MustRegister` |
| `pkg/image/cache.go` | Import `"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"`, call `metrics.ImageCacheHitsTotal.With(prometheus.Labels{"image_ref": imageReference}).Inc()` on cache hit |

## Commands to run

```bash
# From the repo root:
go build ./...        # verify compilation
go vet ./...          # static analysis
make test             # run tests (if available)
```

No new files are needed. The metric is a `*prometheus.CounterVec` (not a plain `Counter`) because of the `image_ref` label, which matches the Prometheus client_golang API for counters with labels. It's registered via the controller-runtime metrics registry (`metrics2.Registry.MustRegister`) following the exact same pattern as the other metrics in that file. The `InitCommonMetrics()` call already exists at the top of `cacheProxy.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet`, so the metric is guaranteed to be initialized before use.
